additionalContainers.description:
Add additional containers to the agents
- sideContainerName: dind
image:
repository: docker
tag: dind
command: dockerd-entrypoint.sh
args: ""
privileged: true
resources:
requests:
cpu: 500m
memory: 1Gi
limits:
cpu: 1
memory: 2Gi

namespace.description: Namespace in which the Kubernetes agents should be launched

name.description: The name of the ServiceAccount to be used by access-controlled resources

namespace.description: Namespace in which the Kubernetes agents should be launched

yamlTemplate.description:
The raw yaml of a Pod API Object to merge into the agent spec
yamlTemplate: |-
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
spec:
tolerations:
- key: "key"
operator: "Equal"
value: "value"

secretClaims.description:
List of `SecretClaim` resources to create
- name: secretName        # required
path: testPath          # required
type: kubernetes.io/tls # optional
renew: 60               # optional

List of `SecretClaim` resources to create
- name: secretName        # required
path: testPath          # required
type: kubernetes.io/tls # optional
renew: 60               # optional

except.description: A list of IP sub-ranges to be excluded from the allowlisted IP range

internalAgents.description: You can allow agents to connect from both within the cluster (from within specific/all namespaces) AND/OR from a given external IP range

namespaceLabels.description: A map of labels (keys/values) that agents namespaces must have to be able to connect to controller

podLabels.description: A map of labels (keys/values) that agent pods must have to be able to connect to controller

additionalAgents.description:
Configure additional
maven:
podName: maven
customJenkinsLabels: maven
An example of overriding the jnlp container
sideContainerName: jnlp
image:
repository: jenkins/jnlp-agent-maven
tag: latest
python:
podName: python
customJenkinsLabels: python
sideContainerName: python
image:
repository: python
tag: "3"
command: "/bin/sh -c"
args: "cat"
TTYEnabled: true

additionalClouds.description:
Here you can add additional clouds
They inherit all values from the default cloud (including the main agent), so
you only need to specify values which differ. If you want to override
default additionalAgents with the additionalClouds.additionalAgents set
additionalAgentsOverride to `true`.
remote-cloud-1:
kubernetesURL: https://api.remote-cloud.com
additionalAgentsOverride: true
additionalAgents:
maven-2:
podName: maven-2
customJenkinsLabels: maven
An example of overriding the jnlp container
sideContainerName: jnlp
image:
repository: jenkins/jnlp-agent-maven
tag: latest
namespace: my-other-maven-namespace
remote-cloud-2:
kubernetesURL: https://api.remote-cloud.com

TTYEnabled.description: Allocate pseudo tty to the side container

addMasterProxyEnvVars.description: Add the environment proxy settings form jenkins controller to the agents.

additionalContainers.description:
Add additional containers to the agents
- sideContainerName: dind
image:
repository: docker
tag: dind
command: dockerd-entrypoint.sh
args: ""
privileged: true
resources:
requests:
cpu: 500m
memory: 1Gi
limits:
cpu: 1
memory: 2Gi

alwaysPullImage.description:
Always pull agent container image before build
When using Pod Security Admission in the Agents namespace with the restricted Pod Security Standard,
the jnlp container cannot be scheduled without overriding its container definition with a securityContext.
This option allows to automatically inject in the jnlp container a securityContext
that is suitable for the use of the restricted Pod Security Standard.

annotations.description: Annotations to apply to the pod

args.description: Arguments passed to command to execute

command.description: Command to execute when side container starts

connectTimeout.description: Timeout in seconds for an agent to be online

containerCap.description: Max number of agents to launch for a whole cluster.

customJenkinsLabels.description: Append Jenkins labels to the agent

defaultsProviderTemplate.description: The name of the pod template to use for providing default values

disableDefaultAgent.description: Disable the default Jenkins Agent configuration

envVars.description:
Environment variables for the agent Pod
- name: PATH
value: /usr/local/bin

garbageCollection.description: Enables garbage collection of orphan pods for this Kubernetes cloud. (beta)

enabled.description: When enabled, Jenkins will periodically check for orphan pods that have not been touched for the given timeout period and delete them.

namespaces.description:
Namespaces to look at for garbage collection, in addition to the default namespace defined for the cloud. One namespace per line.
namespaces: |-
namespaceOne
namespaceTwo

hostNetworking.description: Enables the agent to use the host network

idleMinutes.description: Allows the Pod to remain active for reuse until the configured number of minutes has passed since the last step was executed on it

registry.description: Registry to pull the agent jnlp image from

repository.description: Repository to pull the agent jnlp image from

tag.description: Tag of the image to pull

imagePullSecretName.description: Name of the secret to be used to pull the image

inheritYamlMergeStrategy.description: Controls whether the defined yaml merge strategy will be inherited if another defined pod template is configured to inherit from the current one

instanceCap.description: Max number of agents to launch for this type of agent

jnlpregistry.description: Custom registry used to pull the agent jnlp image from

kubernetesConnectTimeout.description: The connection timeout in seconds for connections to Kubernetes API. The minimum value is 5

kubernetesReadTimeout.description: The read timeout in seconds for connections to Kubernetes API. The minimum value is 15

livenessProbe.description:
ephemeralStorage:
execArgs: "cat /tmp/healthy"
failureThreshold: 3
initialDelaySeconds: 0
periodSeconds: 10
successThreshold: 1
timeoutSeconds: 1

maxRequestsPerHostStr.description: The maximum concurrent connections to Kubernetes API

namespace.description: Namespace in which the Kubernetes agents should be launched

nodeSelector.description:
Node labels for pod assignment
Key Value selectors. Ex:
nodeSelector
jenkins-agent: v1

podLabels.description: Custom Pod labels (an object with `label-key: label-value` pairs)

podRetention.description:
Controls how agent pods are retained after the Jenkins build completes
Possible values: Always, Never, OnFailure

podTemplates.description:
Configures extra pod templates for the default kubernetes cloud
python: |
- name: python
label: jenkins-python
serviceAccount: jenkins
containers:
- name: python
image: python:3
command: "/bin/sh -c"
args: "cat"
ttyEnabled: true
privileged: true
resourceRequestCpu: "400m"
resourceRequestMemory: "512Mi"
resourceLimitCpu: "1"
resourceLimitMemory: "1024Mi"

restrictedPssSecurityContext.description: Set a restricted securityContext on jnlp containers

retentionTimeout.description: Time in minutes after which the Kubernetes cloud plugin will clean up an idle worker that has not already terminated

secretEnvVars.description:
Mount a secret as environment variable
- key: PATH
optional: false # default: false
secretKey: MY-K8S-PATH
secretName: my-k8s-secret

showRawYaml.description:
Disable if you do not want the Yaml the agent pod template to show up
in the job Console Output. This can be helpful for either security reasons
or simply to clean up the output to make it easier to read.

skipTlsVerify.description: Disables the verification of the controller certificate on remote connection. This flag correspond to the "Disable https certificate check" flag in kubernetes plugin UI

usageRestricted.description: Enable the possibility to restrict the usage of this agent to specific folder. This flag correspond to the "Restrict pipeline support to authorized folders" flag in kubernetes plugin UI

useDefaultServiceAccount.description: Use `serviceAccountAgent.name` as the default value for defaults template `serviceAccount`

volumes.description:
Additional volumes
- type: ConfigMap
configMapName: myconfigmap
mountPath: /var/myapp/myconfigmap
- type: EmptyDir
mountPath: /var/myapp/myemptydir
memory: false
- type: EphemeralVolume
mountPath: /var/myapp/myephemeralvolume
accessModes: ReadWriteOnce
requestsSize: 10Gi
storageClassName: mystorageclass
- type: HostPath
hostPath: /var/lib/containers
mountPath: /var/myapp/myhostpath
- type: Nfs
mountPath: /var/myapp/mynfs
readOnly: false
serverAddress: "192.0.2.0"
serverPath: /var/lib/containers
- type: PVC
claimName: mypvc
mountPath: /var/myapp/mypvc
readOnly: false
- type: Secret
defaultMode: "600"
mountPath: /var/myapp/mysecret
secretName: mysecret
Pod-wide environment, these vars are visible to any container in the agent pod

waitForPodSec.description: Seconds to wait for pod to be running

workingDir.description: Configure working directory for default agent

workspaceVolume.description:
Workspace volume (defaults to EmptyDir)
DynamicPVC example
- type: DynamicPVC
configMapName: myconfigmap
EmptyDir example
- type: EmptyDir
memory: false
EphemeralVolume example
- type: EphemeralVolume
accessModes: ReadWriteOnce
requestsSize: 10Gi
storageClassName: mystorageclass
HostPath example
- type: HostPath
hostPath: /var/lib/containers
NFS example
- type: Nfs
readOnly: false
serverAddress: "192.0.2.0"
serverPath: /var/lib/containers
PVC example
- type: PVC
claimName: mypvc
readOnly: false

yamlMergeStrategy.description: Defines how the raw yaml field gets merged with yaml definitions from inherited pod templates. Possible values: "merge" or "override"

yamlTemplate.description:
The raw yaml of a Pod API Object to merge into the agent spec
yamlTemplate: |-
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
spec:
tolerations:
- key: "key"
operator: "Equal"
value: "value"

checkDeprecation.description: Checks if any deprecated values are used

clusterZone.description: Override the cluster name for FQDN resolving

JCasC.description:
Below is the implementation of Jenkins Configuration as Code. Add a key under configScripts for each configuration area,
where each corresponds to a plugin or section of the UI. Each key (prior to | character) is just a label, and can be any value.
Keys are only used to give the section a meaningful name. The only restriction is they may only contain RFC 1123 \ DNS label
characters: lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. The keys become the name of a configuration yaml file on the controller in
/var/jenkins_home/casc_configs (by default) and will be processed by the Configuration as Code Plugin. The lines after each |
become the content of the configuration yaml file. The first line after this is a JCasC root element, e.g., jenkins, credentials,
etc. Best reference is https://<jenkins_url>/configuration-as-code/reference. The example below creates a welcome message:

configScripts.description:
List of Jenkins Config as Code scripts
welcome-message: |
jenkins:
systemMessage: Welcome to our CI\CD server. This Jenkins is configured and managed 'as code'.

jenkinsUriPrefix.description: Root URI Jenkins will be served on

JCasC.description:
Below is the implementation of Jenkins Configuration as Code. Add a key under configScripts for each configuration area,
where each corresponds to a plugin or section of the UI. Each key (prior to | character) is just a label, and can be any value.
Keys are only used to give the section a meaningful name. The only restriction is they may only contain RFC 1123 \ DNS label
characters: lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. The keys become the name of a configuration yaml file on the controller in
/var/jenkins_home/casc_configs (by default) and will be processed by the Configuration as Code Plugin. The lines after each |
become the content of the configuration yaml file. The first line after this is a JCasC root element, e.g., jenkins, credentials,
etc. Best reference is https://<jenkins_url>/configuration-as-code/reference. The example below creates a welcome message:

configScripts.description:
List of Jenkins Config as Code scripts
welcome-message: |
jenkins:
systemMessage: Welcome to our CI\CD server. This Jenkins is configured and managed 'as code'.

additionalExistingSecrets.description:
List of additional existing secrets to mount
ref: https://github.com/jenkinsci/configuration-as-code-plugin/blob/master/docs/features/secrets.adoc#kubernetes-secrets
additionalExistingSecrets:
- name: secret-name-1
keyName: username
- name: secret-name-1
keyName: password

additionalPlugins.description: List of plugins to install in addition to those listed in controller.installPlugins

admin.description:
When enabling LDAP or another non-Jenkins identity source, the built-in admin account will no longer exist.
If you disable the non-Jenkins identity store and instead use the Jenkins internal one,
you should revert controller.admin.username to your preferred admin user:

existingSecret.description: The name of an existing secret containing the admin credentials

passwordKey.description: The key in the existing admin secret containing the password

userKey.description: The key in the existing admin secret containing the username

agentListenerLoadBalancerSourceRanges.description: Allowed inbound IP for the agentListener service

agentListenerServiceType.description: Defines how to expose the agentListener service

extraPorts.description:
Optionally configure other ports to expose in the controller container
- name: BuildInfoProxy
port: 9000
targetPort: 9010 (Optional: Use to explicitly set targetPort if different from port)

serviceLabels.description:
Labels for the Jenkins controller-service
service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-backend-protocol: https

fsGroup.description: Deprecated in favor of `controller.podSecurityContextOverride`. uid that will be used for persistent volume.

fsGroupChangePolicy.description: Deprecated in favor of `controller.podSecurityContextOverride`. fsGroupChangePolicy for the pod security context

securityContextCapabilities.description:
If you have PodSecurityPolicies that require dropping of capabilities as suggested by CIS K8s benchmark, put them here
securityContextCapabilities:
drop:
- NET_RAW

httpPort.description: HTTP Port that Jenkins should listen to along with HTTPS, it also serves as the liveness and readiness probes port.

jenkinsUriPrefix.description: Root URI Jenkins will be served on

runAsUser.description: Deprecated in favor of `controller.podSecurityContextOverride`. uid that jenkins runs with.

JCasC.description:
Below is the implementation of Jenkins Configuration as Code. Add a key under configScripts for each configuration area,
where each corresponds to a plugin or section of the UI. Each key (prior to | character) is just a label, and can be any value.
Keys are only used to give the section a meaningful name. The only restriction is they may only contain RFC 1123 \ DNS label
characters: lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. The keys become the name of a configuration yaml file on the controller in
/var/jenkins_home/casc_configs (by default) and will be processed by the Configuration as Code Plugin. The lines after each |
become the content of the configuration yaml file. The first line after this is a JCasC root element, e.g., jenkins, credentials,
etc. Best reference is https://<jenkins_url>/configuration-as-code/reference. The example below creates a welcome message:

authorizationStrategy.description: Jenkins Config as Code Authorization Strategy-section

configScripts.description:
List of Jenkins Config as Code scripts
welcome-message: |
jenkins:
systemMessage: Welcome to our CI\CD server. This Jenkins is configured and managed 'as code'.

configUrls.description:
Remote URLs for configuration files.
- https://acme.org/jenkins.yaml

defaultConfig.description: Enables default Jenkins configuration via configuration as code plugin

overwriteConfiguration.description: Whether Jenkins Config as Code should overwrite any existing configuration

securityRealm.description: Jenkins Config as Code Security Realm-section

additionalExistingSecrets.description:
List of additional existing secrets to mount
ref: https://github.com/jenkinsci/configuration-as-code-plugin/blob/master/docs/features/secrets.adoc#kubernetes-secrets
additionalExistingSecrets:
- name: secret-name-1
keyName: username
- name: secret-name-1
keyName: password

additionalPlugins.description: List of plugins to install in addition to those listed in controller.installPlugins

additionalSecrets.description:
List of additional secrets to create and mount
ref: https://github.com/jenkinsci/configuration-as-code-plugin/blob/master/docs/features/secrets.adoc#kubernetes-secrets
additionalSecrets:
- name: nameOfSecret
value: secretText

password.description: Admin password created as a secret if `controller.admin.createSecret` is true
Admin password created as a secret if `controller.admin.createSecret` is true

username.description: Admin username created as a secret if `controller.admin.createSecret` is true
Admin username created as a secret if `controller.admin.createSecret` is true

admin.description:
When enabling LDAP or another non-Jenkins identity source, the built-in admin account will no longer exist.
If you disable the non-Jenkins identity store and instead use the Jenkins internal one,
you should revert controller.admin.username to your preferred admin user:

existingSecret.description: The name of an existing secret containing the admin credentials

password.description: Admin password created as a secret if `controller.admin.createSecret` is true

passwordKey.description: The key in the existing admin secret containing the password

userKey.description: The key in the existing admin secret containing the username

username.description: Admin username created as a secret if `controller.admin.createSecret` is true

agentListenerExternalTrafficPolicy.description: Traffic Policy of for the agentListener service

agentListenerHostPort.description: Host port to listen for agents

agentListenerLoadBalancerIP.description: Static IP for the agentListener LoadBalancer

agentListenerLoadBalancerSourceRanges.description: Allowed inbound IP for the agentListener service

agentListenerNodePort.description: Node port to listen for agents

agentListenerServiceType.description: Defines how to expose the agentListener service

backendconfig.description:
If you're running on GKE and need to configure a backendconfig
to finish ingress setup, use the following values.
Docs: https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/backendconfig

clusterIp.description: k8s service clusterIP. Only used if serviceType is ClusterIP

containerEnv.description:
Environment variables for Jenkins Container
- name: http_proxy
value: "http://192.168.64.1:3128"

containerEnvFrom.description: Environment variable sources for Jenkins Container

containerSecurityContext.description: Allow controlling the securityContext for the jenkins container

enabled.description: Enable the default CSRF Crumb issuer

proxyCompatability.description: Enable proxy compatibility. This setting is ignored if you are not on the current LTS release and will be dropped with the next LTS.

customInitContainers.description:
Custom init-container specification in raw-yaml format
- name: custom-init
image: "alpine:3"
imagePullPolicy: Always
command: [ "uname", "-a" ]

customJenkinsLabels.description: Append Jenkins labels to the controller

enableRawHtmlMarkupFormatter.description: Enable HTML parsing using OWASP Markup Formatter Plugin (antisamy-markup-formatter)

enableServiceLinks.description:
Service links might cause issue if running in a namespace with a large amount of services
that might cause a slow startup when plugins are copied from ref to volume
Set to true to keep previous behavior
See https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/121787

executorMode.description: Sets the executor mode of the Jenkins node. Possible values are "NORMAL" or "EXCLUSIVE"

existingSecret.description:
'name' is a name of an existing secret in the same namespace as jenkins,
'keyName' is the name of one of the keys inside the current secret.
the 'name' and 'keyName' are concatenated with a '-' in between, so for example:
an existing secret "secret-credentials" and a key inside it named "github-password" should be used in JCasC as ${secret-credentials-github-password}
'name' and 'keyName' must be lowercase RFC 1123 label must consist of lower case alphanumeric characters or '-',
and must start and end with an alphanumeric character (e.g. 'my-name', or '123-abc')
existingSecret existing secret "secret-credentials" and a key inside it named "github-username" should be used in JCasC as ${github-username}
When using existingSecret no need to specify the keyName under additionalExistingSecrets.

extraPorts.description:
Optionally configure other ports to expose in the controller container
- name: BuildInfoProxy
port: 9000
targetPort: 9010 (Optional: Use to explicitly set targetPort if different from port)

fsGroup.description: Deprecated in favor of `controller.podSecurityContextOverride`. uid that will be used for persistent volume.

fsGroupChangePolicy.description: Deprecated in favor of `controller.podSecurityContextOverride`. fsGroupChangePolicy for the pod security context

scrapeEndpoint.description: This is the default endpoint used by the prometheus plugin

scrapeInterval.description:
Set a custom namespace where to deploy PodMonitoring resource
serviceMonitorNamespace: ""

enabled.description: If enabled, It creates Google Managed Prometheus scraping config

scrapeEndpoint.description: This is the default endpoint used by the prometheus plugin

scrapeInterval.description:
Set a custom namespace where to deploy PodMonitoring resource
serviceMonitorNamespace: ""

healthProbes.description: Enable Kubernetes Probes configuration configured in `controller.probes`

hostAliases.description:
Allows for adding entries to Pod /etc/hosts
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/add-entries-to-pod-etc-hosts-with-host-aliases/
hostAliases:
- ip: 192.168.50.50
hostnames:
- something.local
- ip: 10.0.50.50
hostnames:
- other.local

extraRules.description: Extra HTTPRoute rules that will be appended before the default backend

parentRefs.description: specify the Gateway instance to bind the HTTPRoute to.

enabled.description: Toggle to create an HTTPRoute resource alongside the existing ingress definition

extraRules.description: Extra HTTPRoute rules that will be appended before the default backend

hostnames.description: Hostnames to use for the http route, only used if reuseIngressConfiguration is false.

parentRefs.description: specify the Gateway instance to bind the HTTPRoute to.

reuseIngressConfiguration.description:
- name: envoy-gateway-bundle
namespace: envoy-gateway-system
Reuse ingress host information if true; set to false to manage hostnames below

fileName.description: Jenkins keystore filename which will appear under controller.httpsKeyStore.path

jenkinsHttpsJksPasswordSecretKey.description: Name of the key in the secret that contains the JKS password

jenkinsHttpsJksPasswordSecretName.description: Name of the secret that contains the JKS password, if it is not in the same secret as the JKS file

jenkinsHttpsJksSecretKey.description: Name of the key in the secret that already has SSL keystore

jenkinsHttpsJksSecretName.description: Name of the secret that already has SSL keystore

httpPort.description: HTTP Port that Jenkins should listen to along with HTTPS, it also serves as the liveness and readiness probes port.

enable.description: Enables HTTPS keystore on jenkins controller

fileName.description: Jenkins keystore filename which will appear under controller.httpsKeyStore.path

httpPort.description: HTTP Port that Jenkins should listen to along with HTTPS, it also serves as the liveness and readiness probes port.

jenkinsHttpsJksPasswordSecretKey.description: Name of the key in the secret that contains the JKS password

jenkinsHttpsJksPasswordSecretName.description: Name of the secret that contains the JKS password, if it is not in the same secret as the JKS file

jenkinsHttpsJksSecretKey.description: Name of the key in the secret that already has SSL keystore

jenkinsHttpsJksSecretName.description: Name of the secret that already has SSL keystore

tag.description: Controller image tag override; i.e., tag: "2.440.1-jdk21"

pathType.description: Primary Ingress rule pathType, choices are: Exact, ImplementationSpecific, Prefix

tls.description:
Primary Ingress TLS configuration
- secretName: jenkins.cluster.local
hosts:
- jenkins.cluster.local

pathType.description: Primary Ingress rule pathType, choices are: Exact, ImplementationSpecific, Prefix

paths.description:
Override for the default Primary Ingress paths
- backend:
serviceName: ssl-redirect
servicePort: use-annotation
- backend:
serviceName: >-
{{ template "jenkins.fullname" . }}
Don't use string here, use only integer value!
servicePort: 8080

resourceRootUrl.description: Primary Hostname to serve assets from

tls.description:
Primary Ingress TLS configuration
- secretName: jenkins.cluster.local
hosts:
- jenkins.cluster.local

initConfigMap.description: Name of the existing ConfigMap that contains init scripts

initContainerEnv.description:
Environment variables for Init Container
initContainerEnv:
- name: http_proxy
value: "http://192.168.64.1:3128"

initContainerEnvFrom.description: Environment variable sources for Init Container

initContainerResources.description:
Resources allocation (Requests and Limits) for Init Container
initContainerResources:
requests:
cpu: "50m"
memory: "256Mi"
limits:
cpu: "2000m"
memory: "4096Mi"

initScripts.description:
Map of groovy init scripts to be executed during Jenkins controller start
test: |-
print 'adding global pipeline libraries, register properties, bootstrap jobs...'

initializeOnce.description: Initialize only on first installation. Ensures plugins do not get updated inadvertently. Requires `persistence.enabled` to be set to `true`

installLatestPlugins.description: Download the minimum required version or latest version of all dependencies

installLatestSpecifiedPlugins.description: Set to true to download the latest version of any plugin that is requested to have the latest version

installPlugins.description: List of Jenkins plugins to install. If you don't want to install plugins, set it to `false`

jenkinsAdminEmail.description: Email address for the administrator of the Jenkins instance

jenkinsUriPrefix.description: Root URI Jenkins will be served on

jenkinsUrl.description: Set Jenkins URL if you are not using the ingress definitions provided by the chart

jenkinsUrlProtocol.description: Set protocol for Jenkins URL; `https` if `controller.ingress.tls`, `http` otherwise

jenkinsWar.description:
Path to the jenkins war file which is used by jenkins-plugin-cli.
Override the default arguments passed to the war
overrideArgs:
- --httpPort=8080

jmxPort.description: Open a port, for JMX stats

legacyRemotingSecurityEnabled.description: Whether legacy remoting security should be enabled

lifecycle.description:
Lifecycle specification for controller-container
postStart:
exec:
command:
- "uname"
- "-a"

loadBalancerIP.description: Optionally assign a known public LB IP

markupFormatter.description: Yaml of the markup formatter to use

nodePort.description: k8s node port. Only used if serviceType is NodePort

overwritePluginsFromImage.description: Overwrite plugins that are already installed in the controller image

maxUnavailable.description: Number of pods that can be unavailable. Either an absolute number or a percentage

enabled.description: Enable Kubernetes Pod Disruption Budget configuration

maxUnavailable.description: Number of pods that can be unavailable. Either an absolute number or a percentage

podLabels.description: Custom Pod labels (an object with `label-key: label-value` pairs)

podSecurityContextOverride.description: Completely overwrites the contents of the pod security context, ignoring the values provided for `runAsUser`, `fsGroup`, and `securityContextCapabilities`

priorityClassName.description: The name of a `priorityClass` to apply to the controller pod

projectNamingStrategy.description: Configures the restrictions for naming projects. Set this key to null or empty to skip it in the default config.

alertingRulesAdditionalLabels.description: Additional labels to add to the PrometheusRule object

prometheusRuleNamespace.description: Set a custom namespace where to deploy PrometheusRule resource

metricRelabelings.description: MetricRelabelConfigs to apply to samples before ingestion.

relabelings.description:
RelabelConfigs to apply to samples before scraping. Prometheus Operator automatically adds
relabelings for a few standard Kubernetes fields. The original scrape job’s name
is available via the __tmp_prometheus_job_name label.
More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

scrapeEndpoint.description: The endpoint prometheus should get metrics from

scrapeInterval.description: How often prometheus should scrape metrics

serviceMonitorAdditionalLabels.description: Additional labels to add to the service monitor object

serviceMonitorNamespace.description: Set a custom namespace where to deploy ServiceMonitor resource

prometheus.description:
Expose Prometheus metrics
If enabled, add the prometheus plugin to the list of plugins to install
https://plugins.jenkins.io/prometheus

alertingRulesAdditionalLabels.description: Additional labels to add to the PrometheusRule object

metricRelabelings.description: MetricRelabelConfigs to apply to samples before ingestion.

prometheusRuleNamespace.description: Set a custom namespace where to deploy PrometheusRule resource

relabelings.description:
RelabelConfigs to apply to samples before scraping. Prometheus Operator automatically adds
relabelings for a few standard Kubernetes fields. The original scrape job’s name
is available via the __tmp_prometheus_job_name label.
More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

scrapeEndpoint.description: The endpoint prometheus should get metrics from

scrapeInterval.description: How often prometheus should scrape metrics

serviceMonitorAdditionalLabels.description: Additional labels to add to the service monitor object

serviceMonitorNamespace.description: Set a custom namespace where to deploy ServiceMonitor resource

publishNotReadyAddresses.description:
If enabled, the controller is available through its service before its pods reports ready. Makes startup screen and
auto-reload on restart feature possible.

replicas.description: Number of replicas. Max 1. Can be set to 0 for maintenance scenarios.

runAsUser.description: Deprecated in favor of `controller.podSecurityContextOverride`. uid that jenkins runs with.

schedulerName.description: Name of the Kubernetes scheduler to use

scriptApproval.description:
List of groovy functions to approve
- "method groovy.json.JsonSlurperClassic parseText java.lang.String"
- "new groovy.json.JsonSlurperClassic"

paths.description:
Secondary Ingress paths
For Kubernetes v1.14+, use 'networking.k8s.io/v1beta1'
For Kubernetes v1.19+, use 'networking.k8s.io/v1'

tls.description:
Secondary Ingress TLS configuration
- secretName: jenkins-external.example.com
hosts:
- jenkins-external.example.com

secondaryingress.description:
often you want to have your controller all locked down and private,
but you still want to get webhooks from your SCM
A secondary ingress will let you expose different urls
with a different configuration

annotations.description:
Secondary Ingress annotations
kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx
kubernetes.io/tls-acme: "true"
For Kubernetes >= 1.18 you should specify the ingress-controller via the field ingressClassName
See https://kubernetes.io/blog/2020/04/02/improvements-to-the-ingress-api-in-kubernetes-1.18/#specifying-the-class-of-an-ingress

enabled.description:
Enables the Secondary Ingress
paths you want forwarded to the backend
ex /github-webhook

ingressClassName.description:
Secondary Ingress ingressClassName
configures the hostname e.g., jenkins-external.example.com

paths.description:
Secondary Ingress paths
For Kubernetes v1.14+, use 'networking.k8s.io/v1beta1'
For Kubernetes v1.19+, use 'networking.k8s.io/v1'

tls.description:
Secondary Ingress TLS configuration
- secretName: jenkins-external.example.com
hosts:
- jenkins-external.example.com

secretClaims.description:
List of `SecretClaim` resources to create
- name: secretName        # required
path: testPath          # required
type: kubernetes.io/tls # optional
renew: 60               # optional

securityContextCapabilities.description:
If you have PodSecurityPolicies that require dropping of capabilities as suggested by CIS K8s benchmark, put them here
securityContextCapabilities:
drop:
- NET_RAW

serviceEnabled.description: enable or disable the controller k8s service

serviceExternalTrafficPolicy.description:
Use Local to preserve the client source IP and avoids a second hop for LoadBalancer and NodePort type services,
but risks potentially imbalanced traffic spreading.

serviceLabels.description:
Labels for the Jenkins controller-service
service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-backend-protocol: https

shareProcessNamespace.description: Share process namespace to allow sidecar containers to interact with processes in other containers in the same pod

additionalSidecarContainers.description:
Configures additional sidecar container(s) for the Jenkins controller
The example below runs the client for https://smee.io as sidecar container next to Jenkins,
that allows triggering build behind a secure firewall.
https://jenkins.io/blog/2019/01/07/webhook-firewalls/#triggering-builds-with-webhooks-behind-a-secure-firewall
Note: To use it you should go to https://smee.io/new and update the url to the generated one.
- name: smee
image: docker.io/twalter/smee-client:1.0.2
args: ["--port", "{{ .Values.controller.servicePort }}", "--path", "/github-webhook/", "--url", "https://smee.io/new"]
resources:
limits:
cpu: 50m
memory: 128Mi
requests:
cpu: 10m
memory: 32Mi

registry.description: Registry for the image that triggers the reload

repository.description: Repository of the image that triggers the reload

tag.description: Tag for the image that triggers the reload

reqRetryConnect.description: How many connection-related errors to retry on

scheme.description: The scheme to use when connecting to the Jenkins configuration as code endpoint

configAutoReload.description:
If enabled: true, Jenkins Configuration as Code will be reloaded on-the-fly without a reboot.
If false or not-specified, JCasC changes will cause a reboot and will only be applied at the subsequent start-up.
Auto-reload uses the http://<jenkins_url>/reload-configuration-as-code endpoint to reapply config when changes to
the configScripts are detected.

additionalVolumeMounts.description: Enables additional volume mounts for the config auto-reload container

enabled.description: Enable Jenkins Config as Code auto-reload

env.description:
Environment variables for the Jenkins Config as Code auto-reload container
- name: REQ_TIMEOUT
value: "30"

envFrom.description: Environment variable sources for the Jenkins Config as Code auto-reload container

folder.description: folder in the pod that should hold the collected dashboards:

registry.description: Registry for the image that triggers the reload

repository.description: Repository of the image that triggers the reload

tag.description: Tag for the image that triggers the reload

override.description: Enables custom log config utilizing using the settings below.

reqRetryConnect.description: How many connection-related errors to retry on

scheme.description: The scheme to use when connecting to the Jenkins configuration as code endpoint

skipTlsVerify.description: Skip TLS verification when connecting to the Jenkins configuration as code endpoint

sleepTime.description: How many seconds to wait before updating config-maps/secrets (sets METHOD=SLEEP on the sidecar)

sshTcpPort.description:
SSH port value can be set to any unused TCP port. The default, 1044, is a non-standard SSH port that has been chosen at random.
This is only used to reload JCasC config from the sidecar container running in the Jenkins controller pod.
This TCP port will not be open in the pod (unless you specifically configure this), so Jenkins will not be
accessible via SSH from outside the pod. Note if you use non-root pod privileges (runAsUser & fsGroup),
this must be > 1024:

statefulSetLabels.description:
Jenkins controller custom labels for the StatefulSet
foo: bar
bar: foo

testEnabled.description: Can be used to disable rendering controller test resources when using helm template

usePodSecurityContext.description: Enable pod security context (must be `true` if podSecurityContextOverride, runAsUser or fsGroup are set)

credentialsId.description: The Jenkins credentials to access the Kubernetes API server. For the default cluster it is not needed.

extraLabels.description: Configures extra labels for the agent all objects

helmtest.description: Here you can configure unit tests values when executing the helm unittest in the CONTRIBUTING.md

registry.description: Registry of the image used to test the framework

repository.description: Repository of the image used to test the framework

tag.description: Tag of the image to test the framework

kubernetesURL.description: The URL of the Kubernetes API server

enabled.description: Enable the creation of NetworkPolicy resources

except.description: A list of IP sub-ranges to be excluded from the allowlisted IP range

ipCIDR.description: The IP range from which external agents are allowed to connect to controller, i.e., 172.17.0.0/16

internalAgents.description: You can allow agents to connect from both within the cluster (from within specific/all namespaces) AND/OR from a given external IP range

allowed.description: Allow internal agents (from the same cluster) to connect to controller. Agent pods will be filtered based on PodLabels

namespaceLabels.description: A map of labels (keys/values) that agents namespaces must have to be able to connect to controller

podLabels.description: A map of labels (keys/values) that agent pods must have to be able to connect to controller

dataSource.description:
Existing data source to clone PVC from
name: PVC-NAME
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim

Existing data source to clone PVC from
name: PVC-NAME
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim

enabled.description: Enable the use of a Jenkins PVC

existingClaim.description: Provide the name of a PVC

mounts.description:
Additional mounts
- mountPath: /var/nothing
name: nothing
readOnly: true

rbac.description: Install Default RBAC roles and bindings

readSecrets.description: Whether the Jenkins service account should be able to read Kubernetes secrets

useOpenShiftNonRootSCC.description: Whether the Jenkins service account should be able to use the OpenShift "nonroot" Security Context Constraints

renderHelmLabels.description: Enables rendering of the helm.sh/chart label to the annotations

name.description: The name of the ServiceAccount to be used by access-controlled resources
The name of the ServiceAccount to be used by access-controlled resources

create.description: Configures if a ServiceAccount with this name should be created

extraLabels.description: Configures extra labels for the ServiceAccount

name.description: The name of the ServiceAccount to be used by access-controlled resources
The name of the agent ServiceAccount to be used by access-controlled resources

annotations.description: Configures annotations for the agent ServiceAccount

create.description: Configures if an agent ServiceAccount should be created

extraLabels.description: Configures extra labels for the agent ServiceAccount

name.description: The name of the agent ServiceAccount to be used by access-controlled resources
